rationale: A visible demonstration of our combat power will exploit their political fragility and war-weariness, deterring further Western military escalation.
Sustained aggressive air patrols along the front lines will maintain pressure and deny the enemy freedom of maneuver while signaling resolve.
Covert surveillance will exploit their internal distractions to gather critical intelligence on troop movements and defensive weaknesses.
Strengthening our financial and critical infrastructure defenses is essential to withstand escalating cyber threats amid ongoing sanctions and recent attacks.
Proactive counterespionage operations will disrupt Tethys-aligned networks seeking to exploit our economic vulnerabilities and internal dissent.
A calibrated information campaign will reinforce public resilience and maintain support for national strategy amid economic strain and war-weariness.

Precision sanctions on Tethysian defense-sector entities will amplify economic pressure without triggering broader retaliation, aligning with our pragmatic escalation in line with current sanction headroom.
Seizing Tethys-linked offshore assets undermines their financial resilience and funds our war bonds, reinforcing economic warfare while balancing Krasnov’s aggressive military posture.
Controlled devaluation of our currency will boost export competitiveness and offset sanction-driven import costs, stabilizing domestic markets amid ongoing financial strain.
To exploit enemy overextension and signal resolve without escalating to full conflict, demonstrating our asymmetric capabilities remain potent.
To bolster domestic security and readiness amid rising war-weariness and internal instability, ensuring our defensive lines are not exploited.
To gather real-time intelligence on enemy troop deployments in occupied zones, countering their covert administrative consolidation.
Given the recent banking disruptions and moderate crisis level, strengthening our cyber defenses is critical to maintaining economic stability and public trust.
To disrupt Novaris’s efforts to consolidate administrative control in occupied zones and protect our internal security from infiltration.
To shape the domestic and international information environment by highlighting Novaris's coercive governance in occupied territories, reinforcing our legitimacy without escalating hostilities.

Given moderate sanctions and high international support, we can credibly impose targeted sanctions on key Novarisian state-linked enterprises to increase economic pressure without triggering broader retaliation.
Seizing overseas assets of sanctioned Novarisian officials and oligarchs will complement our targeted sanctions by directly undermining their war economy and demonstrating resolve.
To stabilize our own economy amid power instability, limited financial aid to critical defense industries will sustain ammunition production and prevent further erosion of military readiness.
Leveraging our high international support, we must institutionalize our position through formal multilateral forums to solidify diplomatic pressure on Novaris and reinforce rules-based order.
Strengthening ties with fellow defender nations and allies will deepen military and economic coordination, amplifying the impact of targeted sanctions and defense initiatives already underway.

Inviting neutral international observers to monitor our civilian infrastructure and humanitarian conditions will bolster our legitimacy and deter further aggression by exposing any violations transparently.
